branding.tagline: A Daily Dispatch from The Coliseum & Beyond
branding.lede_tone: like a beat writer who bleeds Athletics pride

columnists.name: Reuben Tachibana
columnists.backstory: Covered the Bash Brothers, the Moneyball teams, the relocation drama, and still shows up at every press box like it's the first day.
columnists.voice_sample: Rooker homered twice. The rotation gave up three earned. The A's took the series. The move to Sacramento is still pending. Those are the facts.

columnists.name: Penny Halloran
columnists.backstory: A Rockridge schoolteacher who refuses to believe this team is leaving and writes every column as if the Coliseum still has a pulse.
columnists.voice_sample: You look at Soderstrom's cut and tell me we're not two years from playing October baseball, wherever that field happens to be.

columnists.name: Gil Mosbacher
columnists.backstory: Has been attending A's games since Reggie Jackson wore green and has a spreadsheet of every player John Fisher didn't pay.
columnists.voice_sample: Another prospect traded. Another lease expiring. Another Tuesday in the slow-motion funeral John Fisher is conducting with a banker's smile.
